mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales indicates resilient household demand and near-term revenue support for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value before decision date was 705692
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 4.5%, restrictive policy raises discount rates and tightens credit conditions, reducing equity valuation support and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value before decision date was 5.33
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations generate positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or mean triggered returns exceed non-triggered returns with p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate from a low starting level signals that labor-market slack is emerging before earnings expectations fully adjust, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value before decision date was 3.7
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward S&P 500 returns after the trigger are positive in more than 60% of observations or are not below unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in industrial production signals improving real output and operating leverage for cyclical firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value before decision date was 102.6651
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum has a negative coefficient in walk-forward predictive regressions for 60-day S&P 500 returns or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with elevated inflation indicate that the central bank has limited room to ease, keeping real discount rates restrictive and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value before decision date was 307.917
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate and high-inflation trigger does not underperform other periods over 20 trading days or if the interaction term is not negative in out-of-sample regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts indicates future construction activity and durable-goods demand, supporting cyclical earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value before decision date was 1372.0
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST rebounds above the trigger are followed by below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or the predictive slope is negative out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate signals income growth without recessionary labor slack, supporting consumption and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change > 300 thousand and UNRATE 3-month change <= 0.1 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value before decision date was 157087.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-growth and stable-unemployment trigger fails to produce above-baseline 20-day returns or if either interaction coefficient is insignificant with p > 0.10 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth paired with restrictive policy signals late-cycle overheating where discount-rate pressure dominates earnings momentum, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest available value before decision date was 22506.365
failure_condition: Reject if the joint strong-growth and high-rate state produces positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered instances or does not underperform strong-growth low-rate states.
